11.intro_text:
The IST Office of Research, Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) fosters collaborative environments among researchers, industrial partners, national R&D organizations, and funding agencies.

Since its inception in 2011, ORIC has been facilitating value-added innovation in both basic and applied research, developing promising innovative processes, products, and technologies according to market-driven requirements.

Our research quality reflects the highest international standards, advancing IST's stature among the world's best research institutions.

gallery_for_api.title: Facilities at IST
gallery_for_api.description: Vibrant student life fostering growth and leadership
gallery_for_api.textfield:
Overview

Going to IST is about making the most of your life as a student. As you get involved, you'll not only have a lot of fun, you'll also develop your leadership abilities, raise your consciousness, and acquire skills you'll use after graduation. Together students, faculty and staff of IST create a welcoming and enterprising community to achieve all this.

List of student societies is always evolving with the changing interests of students. Are you interested in academics, athletics, socializing, social work, performing arts, gaming or movies? There's a good chance that a student society exists for what you want to do.

Wellness and Counseling Center

Wellness and Counseling Center, IST is dedicated to help students, faculty and staff members maximize their sense of overall well-being and facilitate them towards making healthy contributions to their academic, personal, social and career development.

The Centre provides free and confidential on- campus psychological counseling services, both in individual or group settings, where the students address their concerns with a highly trained mental health clinician in a safe and non-judgmental environment.

The Centre further aims to highlight social stigma and spread awareness regarding various topics of mental health through workshops, advocacy and outreach programs.
For your convenience, there are many ways to book a counseling appointment for your professional and personal concerns with either a male or a female therapist,

  1. You can email our psychologists directly at annum.tanweer@ist.edu.pk or taj.iqbal@ist.edu.pk
  2. You can call directly at 051-9075859 between 9 a.m to 12 p.m - Monday to Friday.
  3. You can visit Department of Humanities and Sciences, IST or Wellness and Counseling Center at Second Floor, Rashid Block, IST.

Housing & Dining

Hostel Registration Form

Boarding Facility

Boarding facilities will be limited to students living outside Rawalpindi / Islamabad area. IST is managing 03 x hostels for boys and 1 x hostel for girls. Students are expected to bring decent colour linen and their amenities with them at the time of joining. Boarding facility will only be given depending on availability of rooms.

Cafeteria Services

The cafeteria services in IST are provioded by Cafeteria Contractor on cash basis. A spcious and well managed cafeteria is available on campus. However, cafeteria services in outside hostels are provided by Cafeteria Contractor(s) and Owner of Girls Hostel for girls, on fixed monthly basis.

Tuck Shop Services

A well-organized Tuck Shop is available on campus which caters students’ needs and serves all kind of beverages and snacks etc.

Dining Timings

Dining timings at IST and outside hostels are as under:-

Working Days

  • Breakfast - 0700 hrs to 0800 hrs
  • Lunch - 1130 hrs to 1500 hrs
  • Dinner - 2000 hrs to 2230 hrs

Weekends / Holidays

  • Breakfast - 0730 hrs to 1000 hrs
  • Lunch - 1330 hrs to 1430 hrs
  • Dinner - 2030 hrs to 2230 hrs

Quality Assurance Committees

A committee headed by a senior Administration member, Representatives of faculty, administration and students is constituted to check / cater the standard / quality of Cafeteria Services on campus and outside hostels.

Laundry Facility

Laundry facility is available at IST campus which caters the washing and ironing requirements of all boarding students. Fixed charges of laundry shop are paid by students on semester basis.

Attendant Services

One attendant in each wing of the hostel would be available for cleaning of rooms and other minor chores. The charges of the attendant will be born by students themselves.

Health & Safety

IST Safety Department is responsible for developing and maintaining a safe and secure environment for the employees and students so that they can pursue their academic, professional and personal ambitions. Committed to serving with professional excellence, we strive to be approachable, reliable and truly service-oriented in the performance of our duties. The department maintains strong, professional relationships with all stake holders to ensure continuous collaboration on emergency planning efforts and familiarity with our campus in the event of an emergency.

The following facilities are available at IST for Safety of individuals and premises during emergency.

Fire Safety Facilities

All buildings of IST have dedicated Emergency Exits and are also equipped with fire extinguishers, Water Hydrants. In addition, some areas e.g. data center are equipped with latest firefighting system (FM-200), smoke and heat detectors.
IST also has an in-house, highly trained firefighting team that is equipped with latest Fire Tenders and other modern firefighting equipment.

Health Facilities

Health care is an important and necessary part of any organization. The true mark of an organization is how it looks after its employees and others.

Institute of Space Technology provides medical cover to its employees and students. Medical cover consists of Professional man-power, MI Room, First Aid Facility, Ambulance for 24 hours and First Aid boxes at different locations. The employees of Institute and their families are entitled to free medical treatment from panel hospitals.

IST is providing necessary Information, Instructions and training regarding Health, Safety and Environment to all at IST Campus.

items.title: Convocation 2025
items.excerpt: A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements!
items.textfield:
A day of pride, joy and celebrated achievements!

The atmosphere was electric today as we congratulated the brilliant graduates of the 18th Convocation of IST. Your hard work, dedication, and perseverance have finally culminated in this magnificent success. Today, we didn't just award degrees; we launched future leaders, innovators, and change-makers.

The entire IST family is immensely proud of each and every one of you. Go forth and conquer! The world awaits your brilliance.

items.title: Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics"
items.textfield:
Dr. Ausima Sultan Malik, Assistant Professor, Department of Humanities & Sciences, delivered a talk at National University of Modern Languages on October 8, 2025, on the topic “Gendered Voices: Exploring Language, Power, and Identity in Sociolinguistics.”

The session went beyond theoretical discussion, focusing on how language subtly shapes everyday experiences. It highlighted the ways in which word choices, silences, and patterns of speech reflect underlying structures of power, identity, and gender often in ways that remain unnoticed.

The event was particularly enriched by the active engagement of the participants. Their insightful questions, personal reflections, and willingness to reconsider familiar linguistic practices through new perspectives made the session especially meaningful. Such interactions reaffirm Dr. Ausima’s continued commitment to research, teaching, and critical dialogue in the field of sociolinguistics.

items.title: Faculty Participation in the Pakistan English Conference - 2025
items.excerpt: Faculty Participation in the Pakistan English Conference - 2025
items.textfield:
Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil, lecturers Humanities and Sciences Department, attended the Pakistan English Conference titled “Rethinking English Studies in the Global South”, at Government College University, Lahore on November 27, 2025.

During the conference, Ms. Qurat-ul-Ain and Ms. Ifrah Jamil presented their research paper titled “Teaching Undergraduate Students: A Methodological Shift from Pedagogical to Andragogical Approach” and highlighted the evolving needs of undergraduate learners and emphasized the importance of adopting andragogical strategies to enhance student engagement and learning outcomes.

Their participation contributed to the ongoing academic dialogue on the future of English studies in the region and provided valuable representation for the Institute of Space Technology at a national academic forum

items.title: 2025 IAF Wrold Space Award given to Chang'E6 Lunar Mission including ICUBE-Q
items.excerpt: Every year IAF (International Astronautical Federation) gives two World Space Awards....
items.textfield:
Every year IAF (International Astronautical Federation) gives two World Space Awards. One in individual category and one in team category. In 2023 Elon Musk got the award in individual category and James Webb Space Telescope team (from NASA CSA and ESA) team category.

This year 2025 IAF Space Award in team category has been awarded to Chang'E-6 Lunar team that includes all Payloads (including our ICUBE-Q). Award ceremony will be held during annual IAC in Sydney Australia.

The aim of the International Conference on Engineering Advancements, Science and Technology is to foster interdisciplinary collaboration among engineers, scientists, researchers, and industry professionals, providing a global platform for presenting cutting-edge research and technological innovations.

Click on link to see details.

items.title: The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST)
items.excerpt: The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a....
items.textfield: The International Conference on Applications of Space Science and Technology (ICAST) marks a transformative milestone in Pakistan's journey through space science and technology. As the largest space event in the country, ICAST unites the legacies of IST’s International Conference on Aerospace Science and Engineering (ICASE) and SUPARCO’s International Conference on Space (ICS) into a biennial global forum of unparalleled excellence. ICAST bringing together visionaries, global thought leaders, pioneering researchers, and industry trailblazers, aspires to redefine the future of space science by fostering collaboration, presenting transformative research, and exploring interdisciplinary space applications with the potential to reshape our collective future.

The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is committed to fostering an all-inclusive and supportive learning environment for its community. In abidance with The Higher Education Commission’s (HEC) Policy on Protection against Sexual Harassment in Higher Education Institutions, IST has a zero-tolerance policy for any untoward conduct that violates the dignity of any individual within the institution.

The Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC) was established on 20 February 2012, for monitoring and protection against sexual harassment of any individual at IST. It has since developed policies and procedures to address sexual and gender-based harassment so that members of the IST community feel safe and secure in a harassment- free environment since they have an avenue to voice their grievances.

For further information about the policies or harassment issues, kindly contact:

Dr. Rahila Naz, Head of the Harassment Complaint Cell (HCC), IST
Room number 212, Block 2, IST
051-9075562
head.hcc@ist.edu.pk

Every member of the IST community has the right to work and conduct learning, research, and scholarship without being subject to any form of discrimination, harassment, or social pressure. The HCC’s investigative process is designed to be confidential and fair. No reprimand will be initiated for filing a legitimate complaint. HCC will ensure the protection of the rights of the complainant and the accused.

Eliminating harassment from educational institutions requires a cohesive effort by its community – the HCC is IST’s spearhead in this endeavor.

content.value: Away from congestion, noise and pollution of the city, at approx. 20 minutes drive from Islamabad and Rawalpindi, having multiple access through Islamabad Highway and GT Road, IST is located in the Capital Territory of Pakistan. This advantageous location offers round the clock accessibility through public and private transportation.

From Faizabad it is about 13 Mins, 19.7 KM drive Through Faizabad Interchange , enter into Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) and continue driving towards Islamabad Airport Continue onto Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) pass through Khanna Bridge ( about 4.6 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Expressway(5 Lane) pass through Koral Flyover (Old Air Port Chok)  and enter onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) ( about 3.06 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) pass through Kaak Pul (Suan River Bridge) ( about 8.1 KM ) Continue onto Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) the road will slightly curve right and you will find Canyon Views-EMAAR ( on the left 2.2 KM ), keep in right lane for the U-turn ahead  Immediately after Canyon Views-EMAAR take the U-turn just before the Islamabad Toll Plaza( about 250 meters ) After the U-turn take the first left turn out from Islamabad Highway (2 Lane) ( about 300 meters ) Stop for security check and then turn right, Parking is straight ahead and IST is on the Left

Road towards Rawat Drive for about 7.9 KMs and take a Slight right onto Sawan River Bridge and continue onto G.T. Road After about 5.8 KMs Turn left onto Islamabad Highway Pass through Islamabad Toll Plaza ( about 360 meters ) After toll plaza take the first left turn out from Islamabad Highway ( about 380 meters ) Stop for security check and then turn right, Parking is straight ahead and IST is on the Left

Space is considered to be the final frontier and world’s best minds take up the most daunting challenges to conquer it.

Fazeel Mahmood Khan, PhD

The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is a premier institution in Pakistan, renowned for its focus on space and related sciences. Here's why IST stands out:

Why Choose IST?
1. Specialized Education: IST offers specialized education in space and related sciences, equipping students to address national and global issues.
2. Research Opportunities: The institute has state-of-the-art research labs, providing students with hands-on experience and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research.
3. Accreditation and Recognition: IST is federally chartered, HEC recognized, and PEC accredited, ensuring its degrees are valued globally.
4. Industry Connections: IST has strong industry connections, facilitating internships, placements, and job opportunities for its graduates.
5. Student Life: The institute offers a vibrant student life, with various societies, clubs, and sports activities, fostering personal growth and development.
6. Scholarships and Sponsorships: IST offers merit-based scholarships and sponsorships, making quality education accessible to deserving students.
7. Global Recognition: IST's programs are accredited under Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), substantially equivalent to Washington Accord, enabling graduates to work globally.

Unique Achievements
1. Satellite Development: IST has successfully developed and launched satellites, including iCUBE1 and ICUBE-Qamar, demonstrating its expertise in space technology.
2. Research and Innovation: The institute is committed to research and innovation, with a strong focus on solving real-world problems.

Career Opportunities
At the IST, we empower students to pursue their passion for space science and technology. Since 2002, we've been producing professionals who meet the demands of national organizations and contribute to the nation's technological advancements.

Thriving Careers Ahead
Our graduates enjoy excellent career prospects, both locally and globally. They're employed in leading organizations, including:
• Space agencies (SUPARCO, European Space Agencies)
• Aviation industry (Airbus, Emirates, Air Blue)
• Research institutes (R&D, academia)
• Government organizations (PTA, WAPDA, EPA)
• Private sector (mobile communication, agriculture, airlines)

Diverse Employment Opportunities
Our multidisciplinary approach opens doors to various industries, contributing to socioeconomic activities nationwide. Our graduates work in:
• Space science and technology
• Aerospace engineering
• Research and development
• Telecommunications
• Meteorology
• Agriculture and food sector

Global Recognition
IST graduates are recognized globally, with opportunities to pursue further education at renowned universities worldwide.

If you're passionate about space science and technology, IST is the perfect platform to launch your career. With our strong industry connections, research opportunities, and global recognition, you'll be well-equipped to succeed in your chosen field.

value.question: What is the application process?
value.answer:
The application process involves filling out an online application form, uploading required documents, and paying the application processing fee.
Online admission module can be accessed by clicking the link IST Admissions

value.question: How many seats are for each program?
value.answer:
Engineering Programs:
Aerospace Engineering: 100 - Avionics Engineering: 80 – Electrical Engineering: 100 – Mechanical Engineering: 120 – Metallurgy & Materials Engineering: 80
Computing Programs:
Computer Science: 100 – Artificial Intelligence: 50 – Data Science: 50 – Software Engineering: 50 – Computer Engineering: 50
Science Programs:
Space Science: 50 – Physics: 40 – Math: 50 – RS&GIS: 50 – Chemistry (Nanotechnology): 50 – Biotechnology: 50

value.question: What is duration of study for each Program?
value.answer: The study duration of each program is 04 years (08 semesters).

value.question: How many times admissions are announced for Engineering, Non-Engineering & Computing Programs in a year?
value.answer: Admissions for BS programs are announced once in a year i.e. in March/ April.

value.question: Does an applicant can apply for all engineering, computing & science programs?
value.answer: Yes.

value.question: What is the eligibility criteria, merit determination criteria & fee structure for BS programs?
value.answer:
Updated information is available and can be accessed by using the below links:
https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=general-eligibility&-scholarship=
https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=merit-determination-criteria&-scholarship=
https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=fee-structure-bs-programs&-scholarship=

value.question: Which entry test is required to apply for BS engineering programs?
value.answer:
IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. Please use the below link for details:
https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=entry-test&-scholarship=

value.question: I have already appeared for entry test but I do not receive result card. What should I do?
value.answer: Please get a print of your result card from website of testing authority and upload with online application form

value.question: Which entry test is required to apply for BS computing & non-engineering programs?
value.answer: IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. Please use the below link for details: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=entry-test&-scholarship=

value.question: Does the institute have affiliation with any university/ institution?
value.answer: IST is a federally chartered, HEC recognized and PEC accredited, degree awarding university therefore it does not require affiliation with any other university/ institution. Please use the below link for details: https://www.hec.gov.pk/english/universities/pages/recognised.aspx

value.question: Does Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) recognize the Institute?
value.answer: IST has been awarded accreditation for its various undergraduate engineering programs at Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), making them fully compliant with the Washington Accord. Engineering graduates obtaining a degree under this category of HEIs are considered at par with world class professionals in the field, and do not require any further evaluation of their degrees for professional jobs or higher education abroad.

value.question: Does National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC) recognize the Institute?
value.answer: All Computing programs offered by the institute are duly approved/ accredited by the National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC).

value.question: What is the medium of teaching/ instructions/ examination/ extracurricular activities at IST?
value.answer: All educational and extracurricular activities such as “Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc” at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language. Therefore, IST graduates may not face any difficulty in performing job/ pursuing higher education at any institution/ university/ organization around the globe where the medium of communication/ teaching/ instructions/ examination is English.

value.question: Does IST have reserved seats for overseas/ dual nationality holder applicants?
value.answer: There are no reserve seats for overseas/ dual nationality holder applicants. All admissions in IST are based on transparent open merit basis.

value.question: Does IST have reserved seats for DAE applicants?
value.answer: There are no reserved seats for Diploma of Associate Engineering (DAE) applicants. However, DAE holders can apply on open merit, competing with FSc Pre-Engineering and ICS students.

value.question: Does IST allow result awaiting DAE applicants to apply for admission in IST?
value.answer: Result awaiting DAE applicants are not eligible to apply for admission in IST.

value.question: Does IST allow pre-medical applicants to apply for admission in BS engineering & science programs?
value.answer: Those pre-medical candidates are allowed to apply for admission in BS engineering & science programs who have already passed additional math papers or appeared for additional math papers and waiting for result announcement. OR by completing 8-week condensed Mathematics course from any university.

value.question: Does IST allow pre-medical students to apply for admission in BS computing programs?
value.answer: Yes, pre-medical students can apply for all computing programs.

value.question: Does the institute provide hostel facility?
value.answer: Yes, IST offers furnished hostel facilities for both boys and girls within the campus, providing a convenient and secure living environment for students.

value.question: What is the lowest merit of last years for BS programs?
value.answer: Lowest merit for each program has remained different every year. Therefore, apply online irrespective of lowest merits of last years.

value.question: What are the Sponsorships/ Scholarships are offered by IST for BS students?
value.answer: Details about the Sponsorships/ Schlarships can be accessed by using the below link: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=financial-aid-and-scholarships&-scholarship=

value.question: Does IST have enough qualified faculty?
value.answer: Yes, IST has enough highly qualified and experienced faculty in the respective areas.

value.question: Does IST have proper lab facilities?
value.answer: IST is proud to maintain well-equipped laboratories that fully support our academic programs. Additionally, we have established specialized laboratories to equip students with technical proficiency and expertise in practical equipment handling, thereby ensuring their readiness for real-world challenges

value.question: Does IST have a library?
value.answer: Yes, IST has a resourceful library with sufficient current editions of books, reference material to support the programs, which is constantly updated. Library is also subscribing more than 14 periodicals and online professional publications.

value.question: What are the prospects of higher education?
value.answer: The IST programs are accredited nationally and internationally and the student shall have no difficulty in getting admissions abroad.

value.question: What is the System of Education at IST?
value.answer: IST follows the semester system. Each program is of eight semesters and each semester is of eighteen weeks duration. There will be two semesters in each year.

value.question: Is it mandatory for students of O-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC before the closing date to apply for admission in IST?
value.answer: Yes, it is a mandatory requirement for students of O-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC and upload with online application form before the closing date to apply for admission in IST.

value.question: Is it mandatory for students of A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST?
value.answer: Yes, it is mandatory requirement if an applicant have passed/ completed A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification. In other case, such students are required to apply for equivalence certificate immediately after the completion/ announcement of results of A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification from concerned boards.

value.question: How students can apply for equivalence certificate in current environment?
value.answer: IBCC has introduced an online system to apply for equivalence certificate. Email address/ contact numbers are also mentioned on IBCC website to obtain desired/ relevant information. Please visit IBCC website https://www.ibcc.edu.pk and apply online for equivalence certificates of O-Level/ A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification.

value.question: How much time is given to new BS students for the submission of final FSC result card/ equivalent certificate if it is not available at the time of submission of online admission form?
value.answer: It is mandatory for all BS students to submit the final FSC result card/ IBCC equivalent certificate on registration day. In case of delay in the announcement of results from concerned board, students are required to submit the final FSC result card/ IBCC equivalent certificate within two weeks after the announcements of results from concerned boards otherwise their admission will be cancelled.

value.question: What if student of FSC/ A-Level/ equivalent level foreign qualification comes to know after the announcement of result that he/ she could not meet the eligibility requirements of IST?
value.answer: Admission of such students will be cancelled. It is the responsibility of such students to immediately inform Admissions Office so that their admission can be cancelled and full fee can be refunded. Disciplinary action can be initiated in case of delay or hiding the result for longer time from IST. In such case, fee will be refunded as per IST fee refund policy.

value.question: Is it mandatory to deposit application processing fee?
value.answer: Yes, it is mandatory to deposit application processing fee in any online branch of HBL otherwise your application will not be processed/considered for admission in IST. Application processing fee is non-refundable in any case.

value.question: How it will be confirmed that whether my application processing fee has been received at IST? Or I have to send copy of Paid Chillan form to IST.
value.answer: Nothing is required to send IST. Candidates are required to provide payment details and upload challan forms/ transaction slip with online admission form. Your fee status will be automatically updated on your application form within next 4 to 5 working days after deposit of application processing fee in any online branch of HBL. You can log-in and check the status.

value.question: Can I deposit application processing fee through online bank transfer?
value.answer: It is strongly recommended to deposit your application processing fee through bank challan available at online admission form.

value.question: What should I do if I have paid application processing but my fee status is not updated on online application form?
value.answer: You are required to email scan copy of paid challan form at “admissions@ist.edu.pk” before the closing date to apply for admission in IST.

value.question: Is it mandatory to attach the documents with online application form?
value.answer: Yes, it is mandatory to attach the desired documents with your online application form otherwise your application will not be processed/considered for admission in IST.

value.question: I have lost one of the documents which are required to attach with online application form. What should I do?
value.answer: Apply for duplicate copy from concerned board. Meanwhile you can download your result card from website of concerned board and attached with your online application form.

value.question: I have lost my FSC (Part-1) result card but I have FSC (part-1 + part 2 combined) final result card. What should I do?
value.answer: Please upload FSC final result card with your online application form.

value.question: What I have to mention in admission form if my guardian don’t have (National Tax Number) NTN?
value.answer: Mention NTN number of your guardian, if available, otherwise leave that cell empty.

value.question: What does it mean by Guardian?
value.answer: A guardian is a person who is looking after you in terms of taking your care, paying your educational expenses etc.

value.question: My aggregate score in 1st year is less than 60%. Can I apply for admission in IST?
value.answer: If you are sure that you will be able to improve your score after FSC, as per eligibility criteria mentioned on IST website for respective program, then you may apply for admission in IST based on your score in FSC (Part-1).

value.question: I have completed FSC/ ICS. Can I mention the overall score of FSC or my merit will be determined on the basis of overall FSC/ ICS marks?
value.answer: You are required to mention the score of FSC/ ICS (Part-1) where required because as per merit determination criteria, merit will be determined based on the marks obtained in FSC/ ICS (Part-1).

value.question: When my application will be processed?
value.answer: If you have deposited application processing fee then please wait. Your application will be processed soon.

value.question: When and where merit lists are displayed?
value.answer: Merit lists are posted on IST website within one week after the closing date to apply for admission in IST.

value.question: Is it required to send hard copy of online application form/ Challan Form or documents to IST?
value.answer: You are required to upload desired documents with your online admission form. Do not send application form or any other document to IST.

value.question: What is the last date to deposit application processing fee?
value.answer: Last date to deposit application processing fee is same as closing date to apply for admission in IST.

value.question: What have to mention by O-Level or A-Level students on online application form where Reference Number is required?
value.answer: Reference Number is mentioned just below the line where obtained marks by the student are mentioned on equivalence certificate.

value.question: What have to mention by O-Level or A-Level students on online application form where Serial Number is required?
value.answer: Serial Number is given on top right corner of equivalence certificate.

value.question: What have to mention by FSC students on online application form where Registration Number is required?
value.answer: FSC students are required to mention registration number issued by concerned board.

value.question: Can I edit information in my online application form after submission?
value.answer: Yes, you can edit/update your online application form up to closing date to apply for admission in IST by using your log-in details. In the same way, you can also upload or replace existing documents which are attached with your online application form. When you will upload new document, the previous one will be deleted automatically.

value.question: Can I access my online admission form on mobile?
value.answer: Yes, you can access/ edit your online admission form through mobile. However, for better experience, please use desktop computer to access/ edit the information in your online admission form.

value.question: I have error in my email address or CNIC number. How I can rectify it?
value.answer: You are required to send an email at “admissions@ist.edu.pk” highlighting the problem which you are facing while creating login or accessing your online application form.

value.question: I have completed my FSc (pre-medical) and appearing/appeared for additional math paper. Can I apply for admission to Engineering programs?
value.answer: Yes, you can apply for admission to Engineering programs in IST based on your score in FSc pre-medical (part-1).

value.text: Al Khwarizmi Scholarships for Foreign Students

content.value:
Scholarships are available for Foreign Students to study in Bachelor/ Master/ Doctoral Degree Programs offered by IST.

Duration of Scholarship:
For BS (Bachelor’s) Students: Upto 04 years
For MS (Master’s) Students: Upto 2.5 years
For PhD (Doctoral) Students: Upto 04 years

The scholarship program covers funds for the following expenses:

  • University tuition fee as per actual
  • Stipend @ PKR 15000, 20000 & 35000 per month for students admitted in BS, MS & PhD programs respectively
  • Stipend @ PKR 50000 per month for scientist/ post doc fellow
  • One time return air ticket

Mandatory academic & non-academic charges like admission fee, endowment fund, library development fund, service charges, safe/ smart campus charges, hostel charges etc. are not covered.

value.text: Admission of Foreign Applicants in IST

content.value:
Candidates willing to apply for admission in BS, MS/ PhD programs offered by IST must:

  • meet the eligibility requirements (BS, MS, PhD) for respective program as mentioned on IST website
  • be able/ willing to pay the semester dues/ allied charges as per fee structure (BS, MS, PhD) given on IST website for foreign Students. All fees and other charges are subject to revision from time to time
  • be proficient in English as all educational and extracurricular activities such as "Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc" at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language
  • obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC/ HEC, Pakistan (keeping in view the eligibility requirements to apply for respective program) before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST. In case any document proves to be false, fake, or fabricated at a later stage, a provisionally admitted student shall be liable to expulsion from the University at any stage or to any other disciplinary or legal action the University may deem necessary
  • obtain No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their Ministry of Foreign Affairs or their Embassy in Pakistan
  • prepare personal statement, in English, should include self-introduction, study and work experience, reasons for application and study plan/ research proposal
  • obtain two letters of recommendation from full professors or associate professors, in English. (Professors’ signature, contact phone number and email address must be put on the letter)
  • be willing to meet/ abide all requirements/ rules & regulations issued by the IST/ HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations/ Departments from time to time
  • possess adequate mental and physical health to continue his/ her course of studies

• Admissions will be announced by IST in national newspapers/ on IST website
  • Foreign applicants meeting the pre-requisites will be required to apply online at IST’s website www.ist.edu.pk
  • Foreign applicants will be required to complete online application form in all respects including the deposit/ online transfer of application processing fee before the last date to apply for admission in IST for further process
  • Merit of foreign applicants will be determined according the merit determination criteria mentioned on IST website
  • Merit lists of provisionally selected BS and MS candidates shall be displayed on IST website whereas, provisionally selected PhD candidates will be intimated through email
  • Provisionally selected BS, MS & PhD candidates will deposit admission dues as per details shared by IST to secure their seat
  • Cases of selected foreign applicants for provisional admission in IST will be referred to Higher Education Commission (HEC) and relevant Government department(s) along with their passport copy, photographs, admission letter, NoC of their Embassy/ Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Student’s Information Sheet for Issuance of No Objection Certificate (NoC)/ final approval as per the policy in vogue
  • Selected foreign applicants for provisional admission in IST will be required to approach the Pakistani mission abroad for the visa and submits the following documents:
    • Application Form (Student Visa Form)
    • Photocopy of Passport
    • Photograph
    • Educational Documents
    • Admission Letter of University
    • No Objection Certificate (NOC) issued by the Home Country to Study in Pakistan
    • Any other document if required by Pakistan Embassy at respective country
  • Joining instructions will be issued to the provisionally selected foreign applicants after:
    • The completion of all admission requirements of IST
    • Receiving the intimation/ NOC from HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations
  • Successfully joined foreign applicants/ students will be required to meet/ abide all requirements/ rules & regulations issued by the IST/ HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations/ Departments from time to time
  • IST reserves the right to make amendments in the policy regarding the admission of foreign students at any time without notice/ cancel or refuse admission to any foreign applicant at any time/ stage without assigning any reason

Incharge Admissions
Focal Person (International Student Office)
Email: admissions@ist.edu.pk
Contact: +92-51-9075100

value.text: Flow Chart - How to Apply for Migration in IST

content.value: Several elite National Research & Development (R&D) organizations provide sponsorships to the deserving BS students of selected programs after test/interview. The sponsorship covers tuition fee and a guaranteed job after graduation, provided all requirements of the sponsoring organization are fulfilled by the candidate.

IST offers merit scholarships to three students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per the existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of admissions merit and semester results.

Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to BS students of each batch of each undergraduate program offered by IST:-

GENERAL CONDITIONS:

  • Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship.
  • Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship.
  • Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship.
  • Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time.
  • In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them.
  • If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’ would be at 3rd position and so on.
  • No scholarship shall be awarded if there is an ‘I’ Grade in preceding semester.
  • No scholarship shall be awarded if there is a ‘W’ Grade in preceding semester.
  • Student should take full load of credit hours in that preceding semester.
  • In case, a student takes any additional/ repeat course(s), his/ her merit position will be calculated on the basis of normal load of credit hours in that semester.
  • In case of semester freeze, merit scholarship would only be awarded after re-joining of the student.
  • Transferred students shall not be eligible for the award of merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they studied with the students of previous department/ program. However, such students shall be reconsidered for merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they will study with the students of their new department/ program.
  • In situations where existing rules & regulations are found silent/unavailable, the decision made by IST will be the final which can’t be challenged anywhere.
  • The contents as stated in merit scholarship policy are expression of intent only. The institute reserves the right to discontinue any portion or make amendments in merit scholarship policy/ rules & regulations at any time without notice.

IST offers merit scholarships to two students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of semester results.

Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to students of each batch of each MS program offered by IST:-

GENERAL CONDITIONS:

  • Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship.
  • Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship.
  • Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship.
  • Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time.
  • In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them.
  • If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’ would be at 3rd position and so on.
  • No scholarship shall be awarded if there is an ‘I’ Grade in preceding semester.
  • No scholarship shall be awarded if there is a ‘W’ Grade in preceding semester.
  • Student should take full load i.e. minimum 09 credit hours in each 1st & 2nd semester and minimum 06 credit hours in each 3rd & 4th semester.
  • In case, a student takes any additional/ repeat course(s), his/ her merit position will be calculated on the basis of normal load of credit hours in that semester.
  • In case of semester freeze, merit scholarship would only be awarded after re-joining of the student.
  • Transferred students shall not be eligible for the award of merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they studied with the students of previous department/ program. However, such students shall be reconsidered for merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they will study with the students of their new department/ program.
  • In situations where existing rules & regulations are found silent/unavailable, the decision made by IST will be the final which can’t be challenged anywhere.
  • The contents as stated in merit scholarship policy are expression of intent only. The institute reserves the right to discontinue any portion or make amendments in merit scholarship policy/ rules & regulations at any time without notice.

Higher Education Commission (HEC) with the support of Government of Pakistan has launched “EHSAAS Undergraduate Scholarship Project” for the qualified students from low income families to complete undergraduate studies (Four-Five-year Bachelor programs) from public sector Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) of Pakistan, Azad Jammu & Kashmir, and Gilgit Baltistan. The principle objective of the EHSAAS Undergraduate Project is to provide assistance to the maximum number of students who have secured admission to the universities in various disciplines but lack of financial means to continue their studies. The needy students admitted in IST are informed to apply for EHSAAS Scholarship as and when announced by HEC.

value.text: HEC Need Based Scholarships for BS Students

content.value: Pakistan has no shortage of talented youth, but in several instances, lack of resources prevents them from realizing their potentials. This becomes more problematic when it comes to accessing higher education. The Government of Pakistan acknowledges these constraints and has tasked Higher Education Commission to facilitate the needy and deserving students through Need Based Scholarships. Scholarships are announced for financially disadvantaged students of as many as 94 public sector universities and their campuses. The needy students admitted in IST are informed to apply for HEC Need Based Scholarship after the completion of admission process subject to availability of funds/seats.

The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) under guidance of the Board of Directors is providing opportunities to the talented but needy students to fulfill their dreams and aspirations and at the same time to become partner in the development and welfare of Pakistan. The PEEF, since its inception in 2009 has awarded more than 374,000 scholarships to students of Punjab and other provinces which are currently pursuing education through public and private sector institutions. Needy students, admitted in IST, can also apply for this scholarship. The details are available at https://www.peef.org.pk/Scholarships_Main.html
Interest free loans are also provided to IST students by National Bank of Pakistan (NBP). The process to apply for loan and other details are available at https://www.nbp.com.pk/studentloan/

value.text: Steps of Enrollment in BS Programs offered by IST

content.value:
Step 1 - Admissions Announcement on IST Website/ National Newspapers
Undergraduate (BS) admissions are announced on IST Website/ National Newspapers once a year i.e. in March/April.

Step 2 - Completion of Online Admission Form Candidates interested to apply for admission in IST are required to visit www.ist.edu.pk  or https://eportal.ist.edu.pk/ to create an online account, read & understand the eligibility & merit determination criteria, fee structure, fee refund policy and other related information. Subsequently, eligible candidates willing to apply for admission in IST are required to complete online admission form by providing desired information and uploading the scan copies of required documents with online admission form.

Step 3 - Deposit of Application Processing Fee
After creating an online account, applicants are required to download the challan form available at online admission form to deposit of application processing fee (non-refundable) in any online branch of Meezan Bank/HBL (within due date). It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email.

Step 4 - Merit Lists/ Admissions Status
Applicants are required to check their admission status on IST website by using the login details. An email/ SMS is also sent to provisionally selected applicants. However, applicants are strongly advised to keep visiting the IST website for updated information.   

Step 5 – Acceptance of Admission
Provisionally selected applicants in respective programs are required to download the admission package by accessing the online admission system preferably through IST website. Admission package contain the offer letter, fee challan form/s, an affidavit (write-up), fee refund policy and other documents required to be submitted for registration in IST.

Step 6 – Reservation of Seat to avail Hostel/ Transport Facility (optional)
After the acceptance of admission offer, on the same page of online admission system, candidates are required to reserve hostel /transport seat (if they want to avail services of IST otherwise skip this section). Transport facility is provided only to the students of Rawalpindi & Islamabad from the designated points. IST have within campus separate hostel facility for male and female students with limited capacity therefore interested applicants can avail hostel facility subject to availability of seats.

Step 7 – Deposit of Fee
Candidates fulfilling the admissions requirements and willing to join IST are required to deposit the fee in any online branch of Meezan Bank/HBL within due date mentioned on challan forms. An email/ SMS is also sent for the confirmation of receipt of fee. It is highlighted that fully automated admission system of IST does not allow a late fee option. Therefore, nonpayment of dues within due date will make you in-eligible for admission in IST and consideration in subsequent merit lists and no request is entertained in this regard.

Step 8 – Welcome to IST on Registration Day
Provisionally admitted students are required to prepare the documents according to Offer Letter for the submission on Registration Day.

value.text: Entry Test for Engineering Programs – Fall 2026 Intake

content.value:
IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. The results of following entry tests are acceptable to apply for admission in undergraduate programs offered by IST. Applicants can appear in any one of the below mentioned tests and can use the higher score to apply for admission in IST. Please note that an applicant must have minimum 33% obtained marks in entry test to apply for admission in IST:

  • NAT which is conducted by NTS in every month
  • HAT which is conducted by ETC-HEC
  • NET which is conducted by NUST Islamabad
  • ECAT which is conducted by UET Lahore
  • ETEA which is conducted by UET Peshawar
  • Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by MUET Jamshoro
  • Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by NED Karachi
  • ACT which is conducted by ACT-USA

Further details related to acceptable entry tests dates & categories are as under:

  • NAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories
    The test is conducted by NTS in all major cities of Pakistan in every month. Interested students are required to visit NTS website i.e. https://www.nts.org.pk/new/NAT.php#vd or contact NTS to get desired information related to the registration procedure, test dates and centers etc.

    Acceptable Dates:
    The results of the NAT conducted by NTS from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST.

    Acceptable Categories:
    • NAT-1E and NAT-1CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs
  • HAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories
    HEC has established Education Testing Commission (ETC) to hold Higher Education Aptitude Test (HAT) for UG admissions.

    Acceptable Dates:
    The results of the HAT-UG tests announced/ available from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST.

    Acceptable Categories:
    • HAT–UG–E and HAT–UG–CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs
  • NET Acceptable Test Dates & Categories
    The result of NUST Entry Test (NET) announced/ available from December, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST.

    Acceptable Categories:
    • NET (Engineering Category), NET (Computer Science) both are acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs
  • ECAT Acceptable Test Date & Category
    The result of ECAT conducted by University of Engineering & Technology (UET) Lahore for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST.

  • ETEA Acceptable Test Date & Category
    The result of ETEA conducted by University of Engineering & Technology (UET) Peshawar for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST.

  • MUET Jamshoro Acceptable Test Date & Category
    The result of Entry Test conducted by Mehran University of Engineering & Technology (MUET), Jamshoro for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST.
  • NED UET Acceptable Test Date & Category
    The result of Entry Test conducted by NED University of Engineering & Technology, Karachi for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST.
  • ACT Acceptable Test Dates
    The composite score of ACT conducted from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 is acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Candidates are required to enter their ACT Composite Score in their online application form and upload the score card available in “MyACT account” otherwise their application will not be processed.

value.text: Steps of Enrollment in MS & PhD Programs offered by IST

content.value:
Step 1 - Admissions Announcement on IST Website/ National Newspapers
Graduate (MS & PhD) admissions are announced on IST Website/ National Newspapers twice a year i.e. in April/ May & November/ December.

Step 2 - Completion of Online Admission Form
Candidates interested to apply for admission in IST are required to visit www.ist.edu.pk  or https://eportal.ist.edu.pk/ to create an online account, read & understand the eligibility & merit determination criteria, fee structure, fee refund policy and other related information. Subsequently, eligible candidates willing to apply for admission in IST are required to complete online admission form by providing desired information and uploading the scan copies of required documents with online admission form.

Step 3 - Deposit of Application Processing Fee
After creating an online account, applicants are required to download the challan form available at online admission form to deposit of application processing fee (non-refundable) in any online branch of HBL (within due date). It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email.

Step 4 - Merit Lists/ Admissions Status
MS Applicants are required to check their admission status on IST website by using the login details. An email/ SMS is also sent to provisionally selected applicants. However, applicants are strongly advised to keep visiting the IST website for updated information.
PhD applicants recommended by the concerned academic department are intimated by Admissions Office through email/ telephone call.

Step 5 – Acceptance of Admission
Provisionally selected applicants in respective programs are required to download the admission package by accessing the online admission system preferably through IST website. Admission package contain the offer letter, fee challan form/s, an affidavit (write-up), fee refund policy and other documents required to be submitted for registration in IST.

Step 6 – Reservation of Seat to avail Hostel/ Transport Facility (optional)
After the acceptance of admission offer, on the same page of online admission system, candidates are required to reserve hostel /transport seat (if they want to avail services of IST otherwise skip this section). Transport facility is provided only to the students of Rawalpindi & Islamabad from the designated points. IST have within campus separate hostel facility for male and female students with limited capacity therefore interested applicants can avail hostel facility subject to availability of seats.

Step 7 – Deposit of Fee
Candidates fulfilling the admissions requirements and willing to join IST are required to deposit the fee in any online branch of HBL within due date mentioned on challan forms. An email/ SMS is also sent for the confirmation of receipt of fee. It is highlighted that fully automated admission system of IST does not allow a late fee option. Therefore, nonpayment of dues within due date will make you in-eligible for admission in IST.

Step 8 – Welcome to IST on Registration Day
Provisionally admitted students are required to prepare the documents according to Offer Letter for the submission on Registration Day.

Admission shall be granted absolutely on merit which will be determined on the basis of marks obtained in the following examinations, and according to theweightagementioned against the respective examination:

For MSc (02 years) Students*:

  1. SSC/equivalent: 10%
  2. FSc/equivalent: 10%
  3. BSc/equivalent (02 years): 20%
  4. MSc/equivalent (02 years): 20%
  5. GAT General/HAT General/GRE International (General): 40%
  6. * These applicants can apply for non-engineering programs only

For BSc/BS/BE (04 years) Students:

  1. SSC/equivalent: 10%
  2. FSc/equivalent: 10%
  3. BSc/BS/BE/ equivalent (04 years): 40%
  4. GAT General/HAT General/GRE International (General): 40%

The result of pre-requisite degree/certificate for admission in any discipline should be officially notified by the respective body/ authority of the university till the date of closing of admission.

  3. Applicants with a foreign qualification are required to obtain the equivalent certificate from HEC before applying for admission in MS/ PhD programs offered by IST.
  4. It is mandatory for Government employees to submit NOC from their organization on registration day
  5. If a sufficient number of students are available only then Program/ Specialization will be started.

100% dues will be refunded in case where student could not attain requisite marks/grade for admission as prescribed by the institute. This policy is only applicable on fresh admissions. However Optional Charges will be deducted as per actual usage of facilities based on 4.5 Month/Semester Basis.

content.value:
Institute of Space Technology (IST), following its vision of establishing state of the art Centers of Excellence which would chart a path across all the academic departments/programs of our institute, established Failure Analysis Center (FAC) in March 2013, which has now been upgraded to a national level facility “National Center for Failure Analysis (NCFA)”. NCFA will not only provide a place for research to all the faculty and PhD/MS scholars, but will also be a resource for providing solutions of materials related services to local industries. This initiative is expected to fill a large void, as services being offered at NCFA are currently not available in the country under a single umbrella. It would support a variety of industries by conducting Material Analysis (MA), Failure Analysis (FA), Structural Integrity Assessment (SIA) and providing Material & Process Advisory Services (MPAS). The facility, while still in its infancy, has already commenced to provide consultancy in the following areas:

  • Failure Analysis of metal parts/components/systems by applying “Root Cause Approach” with the aim to ensure avoidance of similar recurrences.
  • Provision of advisory, services or both to the desirous manufactures for:
    • Identifying/selecting metals & processes (including alternative solutions) essentially required for developing parts/components either afresh or through reverse engineering.
    • Inspection of raw materials, on site processing of materials (including heat & surface treatments) and the finished items.
  • Estimation of the status of parts/components/systems with view to:
    • Recommend necessary measures for their restoration (if required & possible).
    • Determine the remaining “safe life period”, for their operational usage.
    • Establish inspection criterion & intervals for parts/components/systems during their “safe life period”.
  • Training of Human Resource:
    • Through formal programs at IST.
    • On-site training on need basis.

In order to provide above stated services/advisory, NCFA has opted to follow modern trends being practiced worldwide in relevant fields/domains. This approach entails:

  • Only essentially required “state-of- the- art” facilities be established in house.
  • A strong pragmatic collaborative network comprising those organizations which already have some authenticated facilities be formed for gaining benefits of their expertise in specific areas and lowering the operational costs.
  • A vibrant advisory board of domain experts (regardless of their geographical locations) be formed for sharing & exchanging experiences
  • Organizations must remain connected & engaged with international community of practitioners & regulators of the domain, for remaining abreast with technological & conceptual developments 

NCFA plans to enhance the scope of its advisory/services gradually in a phased manner.

To establish a world class facility for conducting root cause failure analysis and provision of materials processing advisory to industry, thereby acting as catalyst for enhancing safety in operations & improving quality standards in design and manufacturing.
Provisioning of affordable materials-related solution to our valued customers by addressing their specific needs.

NCFA believes in delighting its valued customers all the way by offering one window, customized, flexible, cost effective, comprehensive, wide ranging and authentic solutions as per their specific needs pertaining to issues/matters related to metallurgy, especially in the field of Failure Analyses. NCFA expects that our high quality and timely delivered services shall be instrumental in establishing long term relations with our valued customers thereby entailing a win-win situation for all.

NCFA fully realizes the sensitivity of its services and their long term legal and safety implications; by default we consider ourselves accountable for all our actions and find under moral obligation to perform all agreed tasks (within contractual terms & conditions) with integrity & secrecy while maintaining impartiality.

This is a systematic process of identifying the factors that caused a failure and the actions that can be taken to prevent or mitigate future failures. RCFA can help you improve the reliability and performance of your equipment, reduce maintenance costs, and enhance safety.

This is a non-destructive technique of obtaining the microstructure of a metal or alloy without damaging the original specimen. Replica metallography can be used to evaluate the condition of in-service components, such as welds, heat-affected zones, coatings, and corrosion products.

This is a comprehensive evaluation of the extent and severity of corrosion damage on a material or structure. Corrosion damage assessment can help you determine the root cause of corrosion, the remaining life of the component, the risk of failure, and the best remedial actions.

This is a process of analyzing the design and functionality of a product or system by taking it apart and examining its components. Reverse engineering can help you understand how something works, improve its performance, or create a new product based on an existing one.

This is a method of estimating the remaining useful life of a component or system based on its current condition, operating history, and expected future conditions. RLA can help you optimize the maintenance and replacement schedules, extend the service life, and reduce the risk of failure.

This is a technique of predicting the number of cycles or hours that a component or system can withstand before it fails due to fatigue. Fatigue life estimation can help you design and select materials that can resist fatigue, evaluate the effect of loading and environmental conditions, and prevent premature failures.

This is an assessment of the ability of a component or system to continue to operate safely and reliably in its current or intended service. FFS can help you evaluate the impact of defects, damage, or degradation on the structural integrity, functionality, and performance of the component or system.

This is an evaluation of the degree and distribution of creep damage on a material or structure. Creep damage assessment can help you identify the factors that influence creep, measure the creep strain and rupture life, and determine the best repair or replacement options.

This is a process of ensuring that a structure can withstand the applied loads and environmental conditions without failure or excessive deformation. SIA can help you verify the design and construction of the structure, monitor its condition and performance, and detect and prevent potential failures.

These are customized courses and workshops that provide you with the knowledge and skills to perform various services related to materials engineering, failure analysis, and structural integrity. Specialized trainings can help you enhance your technical competence, professional development, and career advancement.

This is a service that provides you with expert advice and guidance on the selection, testing, and application of materials for your specific needs and requirements. Material advisory can help you choose the best materials for your project, optimize their properties and performance, and solve any material-related problems.

NCFA is located at the main campus of IST.

Address:
National Center For Failure Analysis
Institute of Space Technology
1, Islamabad Highway, Near CDA Toll Plaza,
Islamabad 44000, Pakistan
Tel No : 051-9075417
Fax No: 051-9075539
Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk, ist.ncfa@gmail.com
Facebook Page: https://www.facebook.com/NCFA.IST
Linked Page: https://www.linkedin.com/company/national-center-for-failure-analysis

Dr Anjum Tauqir

Director
Dr Anjum Tauqir did his masters in metallurgical engineering from Moscow Institute of Steel and Alloys and PhD from University of Connecticut. He has more than 30 years of experience in energy beams-materials interaction, development of hi-purity aerospace alloys and failure analysis. He has his credit more than 80 publications in reputed journals, international conferences and two patents registered in UK. He has established multi-billion semi industrial hi-tech projects in the field of metallurgy-taking them from planning to production. Presently he is professor at Materials Science and Engineering Department, Institute of Materials Science, Islamabad and as Advisor in NCFA.

Contact: +92 051 907 5678
Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk

Dr Syed Wilayat Hussain

Advisor
Dr Syed Wilayat Husain did his BS in Metallurgical Engineering from Dawood College, Karachi. He got his MS and PhD in Metallurgy from the University of Connecticut, USA. He has more than 30 years of experience in Alloy Development, Materials Characterization and Failure Analysis.  He has 50 plus international publications to his credit and has remained associated with projects of national importance. Presently he is a Professor in the Department of Materials Science & Engineering at IST. He is currently also employed as Advisor in NCFA.

Engr Obaid Ur Rauf

Project Lead
Engr Obaid Ur Rauf did his Bachelors and Masters degree in Materials Science & Engineering from Institute of Space Technology (IST), Islamabad and has specialized in Metals & Alloys. His research interests includes Failure Analysis of Aerospace Materials and Advanced Composite Materials, Root Cause Failure Analysis, Non-Destructive Testing and On-site/ Replica Metallography. Currently he is working as Project Lead in NCFA.

Contact: +92 051 907 5734
Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk

Engr Waseem Arshad

Project Lead
Engr Waseem Arshad got his Masters in Mechanical Engineering from SMME NUST and Bachelors in Materials Engineering from SCME NUST. He has been involved in various industrial projects. His key areas of interest are Root Cause Failure Analysis, Remaining Life Assessment (as per API 579), In-situ/ Replica Metallography, Metal and Alloys, Manufacturing, Non-destructive Testing, Engine Tribology and Reverse Engineering. Currently he is working as Project Lead in NCFA.

Contact: +92 051 907 5734
Email: ncfa@ist.edu.pk

Engr Umer Rasheed

Graduate Trainee Engineer – GTE
Engr Umer Rasheed serves as a Graduate Trainee Engineer at NCFA, holding a BS in Materials Science and Engineering from the Institute of Space Technology, Islamabad. With experience in Root Cause Failure Analysis, various Metallurgical Assessments, Remaining Life Assessment, and On-site/Replica Metallography, his contributions underscore NCFA's commitment to excellence in material performance and process reliability.

Engr Mirz Qasim Shehzad

Graduate Trainee Engineer – GTE
Engr. Mirza Qasim Shehzad completed his bachelors degree in Materials Science & Engineering from Institute of Space Technology, Islamabad. He has experience in Root Cause Failure Analysis, Remaining Life Assessment and Replica Metallography. His contributions underscore NCFA's commitment to excellence in material performance & process reliability. He is currently working as Graduate Trainee Engineer at NCFA.
